steps.title: Identify the claim and what evidence must show
steps.content: The student claims Tobar applies journalism techniques to fiction creation. The supporting quotation must show Tobar actively using journalism methods — interviewing, reporting, researching — while working on a fictional work.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — "For one novel, an imagined account of a real person's global travels, Tobar approached his subject like a reporter, interviewing people the man had met along the way and researching the man's own writings.": Shows Tobar using journalist methods (interviewing, researching) to write a novel — journalism applied to fiction. ✓
  • Choice B — "Tobar got his start as a volunteer for El Tecolote, a community newspaper in San Francisco, and wrote for newspapers for years before earning a degree in creative writing and starting to publish works of fiction.": Chronicles his separate career phases; says nothing about journalism techniques crossing into fiction. ✗
  • Choice C — "Many of Tobar's notable nonfiction articles are marked by the writer's use of techniques usually associated with fiction, such as complex narrative structures and the incorporation of symbolism.": Reverses the direction — fiction techniques in nonfiction, not journalism techniques in fiction. ✗
  • Choice D — "The protagonist of Tobar's third novel is a man who wants to be a novelist and keeps notes about interesting people he encounters so he can use them when developing characters for his stories.": Describes a fictional character’s behavior, not Tobar’s own writing process. ✗
That matches Choice A — "For one novel, an imagined account of a real person's global travels, Tobar approached his subject like a reporter, interviewing people the man had met along the way and researching the man's own writings.".
